top_signals.title: Morgan Stanley Australia CEO on Property Market Outlook
top_signals.summary: Richard Wagner, CEO of Morgan Stanley Australia, said auction clearance rates and other metrics are "quite alarming," which may suggest a downward pressure for pockets of the property market. He spoke to Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The May inflation numbers are due out Wednesday morning. Here's what to expect
top_signals.summary: If the Wall Street consensus is correct, the consumer price index is expected to show inflation running at a 4.2% annual rate.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: World's Largest Battery Maker Bets Big on Energy Storage
top_signals.summary: The world’s top battery manufacturer, CATL, expects sales of battery storage systems to account for half of its total sales in the coming years, signaling strong optimism about the battery storage sector as countries investing in alternative energy seek to make it more reliable. Storage is the only way to use electricity later than the moment it was generated, which is how electricity is normally used. With regions such as Europe and parts of Asia betting big on wind and solar electricity, battery storage has become a necessity. China, CATL’s…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
